verses.text: David sends envoys to comfort the new Ammonite king on his father's death, meaning only kindness. But suspicious counsellors persuade Hanun the comforters are spies. A distrustful heart reads malice into mercy, and so turns a friend into an enemy without cause.

David sends envoys to comfort the new Ammonite king on his father's death, meaning only kindness. But suspicious counsellors persuade Hanun the comforters are spies. A distrustful heart reads malice into mercy, and so turns a friend into an enemy without cause.

David sends envoys to comfort the new Ammonite king on his father's death, meaning only kindness. But suspicious counsellors persuade Hanun the comforters are spies. A distrustful heart reads malice into mercy, and so turns a friend into an enemy without cause.

Hanun humiliates the envoys, shaving their beards and cutting off their garments, and sends them away in shame. David tenderly tells them to wait at Jericho till their beards grow. A good king shields his servants from disgrace; where cruelty exposed, kindness covers.

Hanun humiliates the envoys, shaving their beards and cutting off their garments, and sends them away in shame. David tenderly tells them to wait at Jericho till their beards grow. A good king shields his servants from disgrace; where cruelty exposed, kindness covers.

Seeing they have made themselves hateful to David, the Ammonites hire vast Syrian armies for war. Rather than seek peace, pride pours out a fortune to multiply its enemies. Sin unrepented does not retreat; it spends everything to entrench itself.

Seeing they have made themselves hateful to David, the Ammonites hire vast Syrian armies for war. Rather than seek peace, pride pours out a fortune to multiply its enemies. Sin unrepented does not retreat; it spends everything to entrench itself.

Caught between two armies, Joab divides his forces and speaks nobly: 'be of good courage... for our people, and for the cities of our God: and the LORD do that which seemeth him good.' Here faith and effort are joined — do your utmost, then leave the outcome with God.

Caught between two armies, Joab divides his forces and speaks nobly: 'be of good courage... for our people, and for the cities of our God: and the LORD do that which seemeth him good.' Here faith and effort are joined — do your utmost, then leave the outcome with God.

Caught between two armies, Joab divides his forces and speaks nobly: 'be of good courage... for our people, and for the cities of our God: and the LORD do that which seemeth him good.' Here faith and effort are joined — do your utmost, then leave the outcome with God.

Joab advances and the Syrians flee, and seeing them go, the Ammonites flee too. The hired strength collapses at the first push. All the silver Ammon spent buys nothing when God is against them; no mercenary host can stand where the LORD fights for His people.

Joab advances and the Syrians flee, and seeing them go, the Ammonites flee too. The hired strength collapses at the first push. All the silver Ammon spent buys nothing when God is against them; no mercenary host can stand where the LORD fights for His people.

The Syrians regroup and bring fresh armies, but David himself takes the field and routs them, until they make peace and will help Ammon no more. The enemy's renewed effort only deepens his defeat. What God has decided to break, repeated attempts cannot save.
